blocks.body: Conditionals help you talk about facts, real and unreal results, precaution, regret, preference, and strong advice.

{"level":"A2-C1","intro":"In this section you will see how conditionals and related patterns work in three main areas: facts and rules, real or unreal results, and regret, preference, and warning.","rules":[{"label":"FACTS","color":"emerald","text":"Zero Conditional shows what always or regularly happens.","example":"If you heat ice, it melts."},{"label":"REAL AND UNREAL RESULTS","color":"blue","text":"First, Second, Third, and Mixed Conditionals change meaning depending on whether the situation is real, imagined, or already in the past.","example":"If it rains, we'll stay in. / If I had more time, I would travel."},{"label":"RELATED PATTERNS","color":"amber","text":"Unless, in case, wish, would rather, had better help you express condition, precaution, regret, choice, and strong advice.","example":"Take an umbrella in case it rains. / I'd rather stay home."}]}

{"title":"1. What This Section Covers","intro":"The first stage of this branch includes ten core topics:","rows":[{"en":"Zero Conditional","ua":"general facts, routines, instructions","note":"if + present, present"},{"en":"First Conditional","ua":"a real future possibility and result","note":"if + present, will + V"},{"en":"Second / Third Conditional","ua":"imagined present situations and unreal past situations","note":"if + past / if + past perfect"},{"en":"Mixed Conditionals","ua":"a link between a past cause and a present result","note":"different time focus"},{"en":"Unless / Provided / As long as / In case","ua":"alternatives to if and precaution patterns","note":"condition vs precaution"},{"en":"Wish / If only / Would rather / Had better","ua":"regret, desired change, preference, and strong advice","note":"attitude and advice"}],"warning":"⚠ In normal conditionals we usually do not put will after if. Choose the form by meaning, not only by the word if."}

{"title":"2. What to Watch For","items":["Ask not only when? but also how real is the situation?","Zero and First stay closer to facts and real results, while Second and Third add distance, imagination, or regret.","Use Mixed Conditionals when the cause and result belong to different times.","Unless means if not, while in case expresses precaution, so they are not interchangeable.","Wish / If only, would rather, and had better look related to conditionals but follow their own grammar patterns."]}
